column1: # RNG-WINDOW CONSUMER MANIFEST — the source-of-truth registry the freeze gate enforces.
# The v45 north-star: every storage slot a pending randomness consumption READS must be
# frozen against player action for the whole [VRF request -> unlock] window. The runtime
# net (test/fuzz/invariant/RngWindowFreeze.inv.t.sol) asserts that freeze over a
# HAND-ENUMERATED set of slots inside RngWindowFreezeHandler. Nothing, until this gate,
# guarded that hand-enumeration against source drift: a new consumer of a VRF word could
# be added and the runtime net would keep passing because it only checks the slots a human
# listed. This manifest + scripts/check-rng-window.sh close that gap. The gate re-derives,
# from source, every read/write of a VRF-word storage variable and fails the build when a
# live access is not registered here (a new/moved consumer to classify) or a registered row
# no longer matches source (a stale entry to remove). When the gate trips on a new access,
# the reviewer MUST classify it below AND confirm RngWindowFreezeHandler's enumerated slot
# set covers the slot it reads.
# This is text-level analysis (scripts/lib/rng_window_extract.py), not a compiler dataflow
# proof. It is deliberately conservative: it surfaces EVERY syntactic access so a human
# re-runs the backward-trace judgement per changed site. It does not by itself prove a site
# is safe — the CLASS column records the reviewed judgement of why each site is safe.
# TRACKED VARIABLES (the VRF-word storage set). Keep in lockstep with VRF_WORD_IDENTIFIERS
# in rng_window_extract.py and with the enumerated slots in RngWindowFreezeHandler:
#   rngWordCurrent          the fulfilled-but-not-yet-sealed daily buffer (own slot)
#   rngWordByDay[day]       the sealed, write-once per-day word            (slot 10)
#   lootboxRngWordByIndex[] the sealed, write-once per-index lootbox word  (slot 35)
#   lootboxRngPacked        the packed lootbox cursor; low 48 bits = index (slot 34)
# FROZEN_SET declares the identifiers the runtime net must enumerate; the gate cross-checks
# that RngWindowFreezeHandler references each. (dailyIdx — the non-VRF day cursor, slot 0
# byte 3 — is a 4th enumerated slot in the handler but is read in hundreds of unrelated
# places, so it is NOT tracked as a word variable here; the handler covers it directly.)
# WINDOW ASYMMETRY (a reviewer note the CLASS judgements rest on): a DAILY request sets both
# rngLockedFlag and prizePoolFrozen; a MID-DAY requestLootboxRng sets NEITHER — it relies on
# the LR_MID_DAY bit + rngRequestTime != 0. So "a VRF request is outstanding" is NOT the same
# as rngLocked() == true. Any future CONSUMER-SEALED/GATE/CURSOR row must be re-checked
# against BOTH windows, not just the daily lock.
#   DECL            storage declaration of a tracked variable.
#   PRODUCER        writes a word/cursor. Every writer today runs in the exempt advance /
#                   VRF-callback / mint-queue path (a cursor-index allocation). A writer that
#                   appears in a NEW function is the highest-signal drift: re-verify it cannot
#                   rewrite a sealed word a pending consumption will read.
#   EXEMPT-ADVANCE  read inside advanceGame / the VRF request-response / seal / gap-backfill /
#                   game-over terminal flow — the v45-exempt heartbeat (no player interleave
#                   between the read and the word it depends on).
#   CONSUMER-SEALED player-reachable read that consumes a SEALED, write-once word for a value
#                   decision. Safe because the word is written once by the exempt producer and
#                   read only after unlock — a player cannot change what it reads mid-window.
#                   not consumed for a value decision.
#   CURSOR          player-reachable read of the lootboxRngPacked cursor — the non-VRF slot read
#                   ALONGSIDE the lootbox word (its own bug class; the freeze net enumerates it).
#   ACCESSOR        pure view getter / packed-field helper (no decision, no window sensitivity).
# COLUMNS (tab-separated): CLASS  IDENTIFIER  FUNCTION  FILE  MODE
# Regenerate the body after a large refactor with:
# then hand-review every row before committing.
